event.name: EWE Cup
event.source: 2026 SCC EWE Spirit Cup Sailing Instructions

medium.notes: Appendix A medium pursuit course, CCW shown. CW is permitted; both turning marks must be rounded to port.

mediumR.notes: Appendix A medium pursuit course, CW option. Both turning marks must be rounded to port.

short.notes: Appendix A short pursuit course, CCW shown. CW is permitted; both turning marks must be rounded to port.

shortR.notes: Appendix A short pursuit course, CW option. Both turning marks must be rounded to port.

specialRoutingNotes: Pursuit Division warning signal is scheduled for 1200 Saturday, May 16, 2026 at GC "7" off Horn Point.
The start lies between GC "7" and an orange flag on the race committee boat to the southwest of the mark.
The finish lies between a drop mark and an orange flag on the race committee boat.
All course marks shall be rounded to port.
Course may be sailed either direction, except the J/105 class will all sail the same direction as announced on VHF before the first warning.
The following marks shall always be passed on the channel side for keelboat classes: "1AH" at Tolly Point, FL R 6s 15ft 4M "4" off Greenbury Point, and FL 6s 15ft 4M "HP" shoal pole.
Race committee announcements are intended on VHF channel 73.
A boat starting early receives a 5-minute penalty without a hearing and need not restart.
A boat not starting within 10 minutes after her starting signal is scored DNS without a hearing.
Any yacht not finishing by 1630 Saturday, May 16, 2026 will be scored TLE.
A retiring boat shall notify race committee as soon as possible on VHF channel 73 or by email at scc1944.rc@gmail.com.
Do not interfere with commercial traffic; propulsion may be used only as needed to avoid commercial traffic if no advantage is gained and a written report is submitted by the protest deadline.
